response:
Short answer: we follow an App‑Gateway‑first troubleshooting pattern focused on WAF and allowlists.

Steps we use when debugging fazure‑mcp traffic issues

1. Check AGW access logs (AGWAccessLogs) for the symptom (timestamps, status codes, client IPs, spikes).
2. Check AGW firewall logs (AGWFirewallLogs) to see which WAF custom or managed rules fired (rule IDs and rule names).
3. Correlate with backend/origin logs to rule out genuine backend 5xxs (i.e., is it a WAF block or a backend error).
4. Verify the client IP is/should be trusted (Rackspace VPN, Janus AWS, etc.) and confirm that the appropriate Allow rule exists in the effective WAF policy (global vs per‑listener/site policy).
5. Inspect per‑listener/site WAF policy artifacts — common mistakes are copying managed settings but omitting global Allow rules or exclusions.
6. If it’s a false positive from managed rules, apply the reusable WAF mitigation pattern: add targeted allowlists, move/change custom rule priorities, add managed‑rule exclusions (or create a per‑site WAF policy) to bypass inspection for known MCP traffic (e.g., exclude ARGS causing SQLi FP).
7. Roll the change live, then re‑test and re‑check AGW logs to confirm the fix.

This is the established pattern used in the April investigations (identify via AGWAccessLogs → confirm WAF hits in AGWFirewallLogs → verify allowlist/policy differences → apply per‑site or rule/priority fixes → validate).

In AGWAccessLogs for fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com over the prior 24h, there were 0 HTTP 500 responses, multiple 502s concentrated around 13:46-13:50 UTC and 14:14-14:15 UTC and especially 14:38-14:51 UTC, and a small number of 403s. AGWFirewallLogs showed trusted Rackspace/Janus traffic matching AllowTrustedVpnFazureMcp and current 403s were mainly expected VpnOnly blocks from external/scanner or preview IPs such as 146.70.189.237 and 52.123.190.89/125. T", "2026-04-14: On 2026-04-14, after new MCP 403s from Janus AWS IPs, added the full Janus AWS address set 52.39.2.74, 52.41.136.221, 52.10.115.132, and 52.40.141.99 to the trusted IP allowlists for fazure MCP and oauth endpoints on Azure Application Gateway. Root cause was that recent POST /mcp failures on fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com at 2026-04-14T17:51:41Z through 2026-04-14T18:06:13Z were blocked by custom rule VpnOnly under policy scope fazure-mcp, even though those IPs are the known JanusAWS set already present in the global policy. Fixed live by updating site policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcent", "2026-03-25: On 2026-03-25, investigated fazure-mcp WAF 403s for POST /mcp from Rackspace VPN IP 72.32.180.181. Local Azure-MCP rollout artifacts showed the per-listener WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax-fazure-mcp-sites was created by copying managedRules and policySettings from the global policy, then attaching only site custom rules RateLimit, VpnOnly, BlockSensitiveFiles, BlockKnownPath, GeoBlocking, and AllowSafeFilters. The global RackspaceVPN Allow rule was not copied into the site policy. Result: trusted VPN traffic still traverses OWASP CRS 3.2 managed rules on /mcp and legitima", "2026-03-25: On 2026-03-25, a reusable WAF mitigation pattern was established for trusted internal MCP SQL traffic behind Azure Application Gateway. Rackspace VPN IP 72.32.180.181 was receiving 403s on POST /mcp to fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com because the per-listener WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax-fazure-mcp-sites allowed the VPN through the VpnOnly rule but still sent the JSON request body through OWASP managed inspection. AGWFirewallLogs showed SQL injection false positives on ARGS:params.arguments.query with rules 920230, 942100, 942150, 942260, 942360, 942370, 942380, 942410,", "2026-03-25: On 2026-03-25, persistent 403s from Rackspace VPN IP 72.32.180.181 to fazure-oauth.mscloud.rackspace.com/authorize were traced to a mismatch in the global App Gateway WAF allow rule for MCP dynamic client registration and auth paths. AGWAccessLogs in workspace ecae2296-e4c2-47f2-9336-24599caea6be showed repeated GET /authorize requests returning 403 between 2026-03-25T14:40:58Z and 2026-03-25T14:41:34Z. AGWFirewallLogs showed custom rule BlockKnownPath blocking the full RequestUri including query string, and managed rule 931130 (Possible Remote File Inclusion / Off-Domain Reference/Link) plus ", "2026-03-25: On 2026-03-25, investigated 403s from Rackspace VPN IP 72.32.180.178 to fazure-oauth.mscloud.rackspace.com/register. Live AGW logs showed OPTIONS /register returned 200, while POST /register was blocked by global WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax with rule 931130 (Possible Remote File Inclusion / Off-Domain Reference/Link) on client_uri=https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/inspector and redirect_uris=http://localhost:6274/oauth/callback/debug, followed by 949110 anomaly score block. Updated global WAF custom rules by moving BlockKnownPath from priority 5 to 6 and adding A", "2026-03-19: On 2026-03-19 after expanding the fazure WAF lockdown, a live burst test from Rackspace VPN public IP 72.32.180.181 against https://fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com/mcp did not trigger the Application Gateway WAF RateLimit rule. Baseline responses from the VPN client were /health -> 200 and /mcp -> 401. A 130-request burst to /mcp completed with 130 HTTP 401 responses and the immediate follow-up request also returned 401. Log Analytics confirmed 132 /mcp requests with HttpStatus 401 in the 2026-03-19T19:39:00Z minute for ClientIp 72.32.180.181. AGWFirewallLogs for the same client/host/path onl", "2026-03-19: On 2026-03-19, live investigation of App Gateway appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax found two independent exposure paths for fazure-mcp/fazure-oauth. First, the WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax is in Prevention mode but only has Allow rules for RackspaceVPN and JanusAWS; it has no catch-all Block for non-allowed IPs. From external client IP 47.221.164.73, requests to fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com/health returned 200 and /mcp returned 401 through the gateway, and AGWFirewallLogs showed zero custom rule hits for that client, proving public traffic is allowed unless anot", "2026-03-19: On 2026-03-19 in /Users/benj8956/Downloads/Azure-MCP, created and applied dedicated per-site WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax-fazure-mcp-sites to listeners fazure-mcp, fazure-mcp-dev, fazure-cosmos-mcp, and fazure-cosmos-mcp-dev using script scripts/fazure_per_site_waf.sh. The site policy cloned the global policy managed settings and exclusions, and applied custom rules: RateLimit priority 1 on RequestUri begins with /mcp, VpnOnly priority 2 blocking non-RackspaceVPN IPs, BlockSensitiveFiles priority 4, BlockKnownPath priority 5, GeoBlocking priority 10, AllowSafeFilters pr"]}
